full_prompt: Design a monolithic Ti-6Al-4V (Grade 5, annealed) titanium front H2 tank chassis bracket for a collegiate formula-class racecar benchmark 2026 H2 Class vehicle running the FS Rules 2026 base (tube OD 25.4 x 2.4 mm steel chassis reference, 95th-%ile driver template, IA 7.5g avg / 20g peak, 60 deg tilt; 20 mm gasoline restrictor does not apply to H2 vehicles) plus the FSA H2 Rules 2026 v1.0/1.2 addendum defining H2 powertrain envelope for PEM fuel-cell vehicles. The bracket is a single billet-machined titanium part (no welds, no inserts, no bolted gussets) that cradles a type-IV 350 bar hydrogen cylinder (Luxfer L45 analog: 5 L water capacity, 305 mm OD x 505 mm length, 5.5 kg empty + 0.2 kg H2 full = 5.7 kg) to the main hoop on one side and the front roll hoop on the other side of the chassis. Geometry: strap-style bracket with a 305 mm ID cylindrical cradle arc spanning 180 deg, radial arc thickness 8 mm, axial length 70 mm; two integral mounting feet at 3 and 9 o'clock each with a flat 50 mm x 70 mm x 10 mm pad carrying two M10 clearance holes (11 mm) on 40 mm spacing for clamping via a retention strap back into the chassis tubing (tubes not part of this CAD prompt); integral anti-rotation stub 20 mm x 30 mm x 15 mm at the 12 o'clock position engaging a matching slot in the cylinder label face (prevents cylinder rotation). Overall bounding envelope 400 x 340 x 90 mm. Material Ti-6Al-4V Grade 5 annealed per AMS 4911: E 113.8 GPa, nu 0.342, density 4430 kg/m^3, yield 860 MPa, UTS 950 MPa, endurance limit 410 MPa at 10^7 cycles smooth-polished (derated 0.7 surface factor for machined = 287 MPa). Three load cases per FS Rules 2026 + FSA H2 Rules 2026. Load Case 1 (40g longitudinal): 40g longitudinal deceleration on the 5.7 kg filled cylinder mass = 2.24 kN longitudinal force distributed through the cradle, both feet fully constrained; this implements the IA-envelope equivalent deceleration requirement even though the IA itself is rated at 20g peak, for fuel-system mass retention FSA requires 40g structural (H2 Rules 2026 section on pressure vessel retention). Load Case 2 (20g lateral): 20g lateral acceleration on the cylinder mass = 1.12 kN lateral, both feet fixed (this matches FS T.3.2 fuel-system retention in side crashes). Load Case 3 (60-deg tilt static + operating pressure): 60-deg tilt per T.3.2.1 with cylinder internal pressure at 35 MPa service giving radial expansion on the cradle equivalent to a 0.15 mm interference fit; model as a radial outward pressure on the cradle's inner arc surface of 2.5 MPa (conservative contact reaction) plus 1.0g tilt load. Pass/fail: (R1) under LC1 40g, peak von Mises must not exceed 430 MPa (yield/2.0 per FSA H2 Rules 2026 retention SF); (R2) under LC2 20g lateral, peak stress 430 MPa; (R3) under LC3 tilt + radial expansion, peak stress 430 MPa; (R4) fatigue: 10^7 cycles of combined LC2 stress range at 30% of LC2 peak, local stress range at any foot fillet must not exceed 287 MPa; (R5) a linear buckling eigenvalue analysis under LC1 compressive cradle loading must yield a first-mode factor of at least 3.0; (R6) bracket mass must not exceed 0.55 kg to limit front-axle mass penalty; (R7) maximum radial deflection of the cradle arc under LC3 must not exceed 0.3 mm to preserve cylinder contact without localized over-compression of the resin overwrap. Thermal, fluid, and radiation analyses are not required. Deliver STEP AP242 and Parasolid plus report.
short_prompt:
Design a monolithic Ti-6Al-4V (Grade 5 annealed) front H2-tank chassis bracket for a collegiate formula-class racecar (FS Rules 2026 + FSA H2 Rules 2026 addendum), cradling a Type IV 350 bar hydrogen cylinder (305 mm OD x 505 mm, 5.7 kg filled) between the main hoop and front roll hoop. The bracket is a single billet-machined titanium part with a 180-degree cylindrical cradle arc, two integral mounting-foot pads at 3 and 9 o'clock each carrying two strap-retention holes, and an integral 12-o'clock anti-rotation stub; no welds, no inserts. Apply yield/2.0 retention SF per FSA H2 Rules.

Envelope: 400 x 340 x 90 mm, 305 mm cradle ID x 70 mm axial length, 8 mm arc thickness.

Load cases.
- LC1 40 g longitudinal (H2 Rules pressure-vessel retention): 2.24 kN longitudinal on the 5.7 kg filled cylinder, both mounting feet fixed; also a linear-buckling factor >= 3 under compressive cradle loading.
- LC2 20 g lateral (FS T.3.2 side-crash retention): 1.12 kN lateral, both feet fixed.
- LC3 60-degree tilt + operating pressure: 1 g tilt load plus a 2.5 MPa radial pressure on the cradle inner arc (contact reaction from 35 MPa cylinder radial expansion / 0.15 mm interference).
Also check a 10^7-cycle fatigue spectrum at foot fillets and cradle radial deflection under LC3 below 0.3 mm to avoid over-compressing the cylinder overwrap.

prompt.title: FSA H2 Class 350 bar tank chassis bracket (Ti-6Al-4V)
prompt.brief: Single billet Ti bracket cradling a 5 L type-IV 350 bar H2 cylinder; FSA H2 Rules 2026 40g/20g retention + 60-deg tilt.

exclusions_explained.thermal_analysis: FC thermal handled at FC stack not the mount.
exclusions_explained.fluid_analysis: H2 inside tank not relevant to bracket.
exclusions_explained.vibration_analysis: Cylinder is strap-retained; NVH not governing.
